subject: TSL Weekly Digest — Week of Mar 9, 2026
html:
TSL Internal

TSL Weekly Strategy

Week of Mar 9–14, 2026  ·  For Sam & David

What Got Done This Week

Product: Homepage shipped — single-screen layout with sourcing left and pitch deck upload right. Fund evaluation framework built out in detail: graduation rates (55–60%+ target), GP commit thresholds (>2%), valuation methodology standardization (three-number approach: pro forma TVPI, last-round valuation, preemptive markdowns). Two-pager identified as key differentiator. Agentic Claude/Cowork integration scoped for continuous task execution. SF Launch Party dinner held — 13 LP/allocator attendees. Charlotte sync on Vantager post-mortem, Burgiss data access, and direct product input. Cendana + Stifel work sessions. NY event locked for April 27.

GTM: New contacts surfaced — John from RBC moved to Northleaf (strong position, 1–2 fund investments annually), Miles left Citizens Bank building a GP/LP ecosystem, JT fundraising for a $50M FoF (15 funds, SMA structure). Sunshine Lake (Don, former Global Founders Capital) flagged as a live inbound — 17 exits from GFC, strong LP roster including Excel Partners family office, YC FoF, Gary Tan, Andreessen FoF, meeting scheduled next week.

Path to First Paid Customer ($10K by May)

What needs to be true: Three things in sequence. First: a working pitch deck upload demo — Charlotte needs to see it by Mar 18, and it needs to produce a benchmarking output she'd actually use (Burgiss data connected, two-pager draft). DocSend extraction is still the critical blocker. Second: a clear $10K ask with defined deliverables — there's no explicit offer on the table yet, which means even a willing buyer can't say yes. Third: April 27 NYC event needs to convert at least one LP from "interested" to "paying." Six weeks is tight.

Did this week move the needle? Product direction got sharper, SF event built real relationships, and Charlotte is now deeply invested as a design partner. But DocSend still isn't solved — which means the demo deadline starts Monday still blocked at the same point. That's the only thing that matters before Mar 18.

Most likely first customer: Charlotte. She has a June KPI to automate LP workflows, direct Burgiss data access, and she's already co-designing the product. Gap: no platform login yet, hasn't seen the demo. Close both this week and she's convertible before April 27. Second candidate: someone from the SF dinner — Adrian (Equity Alliance, first year deploying at $500K–1M checks), Max (S Cubed, Mark Stevens FO), or Alex (Jordan Park, $2.5B AUM) are the highest-signal names. Third: Sunshine Lake (Don) is a fresh inbound who should see the pitch.

Alignment Check

Aligned: Product vision is clear — two-pager as differentiator, community intelligence over pure data, single-screen homepage, hide everything that isn't core. SF event and Charlotte sync both reinforced this direction.

Friction to surface: What exactly is the $10K offer? Sam is building toward a demo. David is running events and relationships. Those efforts converge at April 27 — but without an explicit pricing conversation before that date, the event generates warmth without a path to conversion. This is worth 15 minutes before the week starts.

Bandwidth & Resource Reality

Sam's plate: DocSend extraction + pitch deck demo (Mar 18 deadline) + Charlotte login + Burgiss data + Shy's fund database + automated onboarding system + Ben/Will intro + Cameron email. That's too much. The only thing that matters before Mar 18 is the demo. Everything else waits.

David's plate: SF follow-ups (Adrian, Max, Alex — momentum fades fast), Sunshine Lake meeting prep, Supabase access for Sam, Marco (VC Lab) connection, podcast logistics. SF follow-ups are time-sensitive and highest-leverage this week. Podcast B-roll and thumbnail work should not be on this list.

Drop this week: Podcast logistics, Supabase migration, bulk fund upload, community events page, analytics tracking. None are on the path to $10K by May. Northleaf (John) and JT's FoF are worth a note but not urgent outreach this week.

Platform Pulse

No metrics discussed this week. Recommended to track weekly: active users, funds researched, pitch decks uploaded, community notes added. Without this, you can't tell if the product is being used or just built.

Competitive Signals

  • Vantager → MSCI: Charlotte received $7K. Acqui-hire, not product acquisition. The GP integration + full auditability gap is wide open — that's the moat to build into the two-pager. Key insight from Charlotte: Vantager's value was end-deliverable memos (not intermediate products) + full click-through auditability. TSL needs both.
  • Velvet: Charlotte rolling off as advisor. Founder distracted, engineering too slow. Not a near-term threat.
  • Erasmus: CTO/domain tension, Benchmark-backed but drifting toward pure tech. Worth watching.
  • Unlimited AI, Cannon Project, SuccessVP: On radar, no new intel this week.

Priorities for Next Week

  1. [Sam] Solve DocSend extraction and ship pitch deck upload demo — Charlotte demo by Mar 18, non-negotiable
  2. [Sam] Get Charlotte platform login + Burgiss data connected before the demo
  3. [David] SF dinner follow-ups — Adrian, Max, Alex while the relationship is fresh
  4. [Both] Define the $10K offer before April 27 — product, pricing, and what "paying customer" actually means
  5. [David] Sunshine Lake meeting (Don, former GFC) — strong inbound, prep and meet this week

Sources: Sam/David Co-Work Mar 13 (×2), Charlotte/Sam Sync Mar 11, Sam Update #TSLinternal Mar 11, TSL/Allocator Ideas Brief Mar 9, Sam/DZ Weekly Mar 9
